2.text_description:
The **theatrical release version of the U.S. poster for *The Dark Knight* (2008)** shows **Batman** standing in front of a tall, dark building at night.

He is seen from **behind**, slightly turned to the **left**, so part of his chest and the bat emblem on his suit are visible. He is wearing the **black armored Batsuit** with a cape hanging down to his legs, and his head is turned forward, facing the building.

The **building** behind him is a **modern skyscraper**, its **windows illuminated** in a grid pattern. Near the **center of the building**, several windows are **broken and scorched**, forming the **shape of the Batman symbol** — a large **bat-shaped burn mark** made of **orange flames and smoke**. The glowing symbol gives off light that reflects faintly on Batman’s back and cape.

The **surrounding area** is **dark blue-gray**, suggesting nighttime and possibly light rain or haze. The **ground level** shows **debris and firelight reflections**, indicating destruction or chaos below.

At the **top of the poster**, the main text reads in all caps:
**"WELCOME TO A WORLD WITHOUT RULES."**

At the **bottom**, the **title “THE DARK KNIGHT”** appears in a bold, metallic font, with the **Bat symbol** placed directly beneath or integrated behind the title. Below that is a **credit block** listing the main actors and production team in small type, and the **release date “JULY 18”** is printed near the bottom center.

No other characters are shown — only **Batman, the burning bat symbol on the building,** and **text elements** as described.

3.text_description:
The theatrical release version of the **U.S. poster for *The Matrix* (1999)** shows the four main characters standing in a group against a stylized, vertically streaked, blue-gray background. The background gives the impression of a wet city alley or corridor, with faint outlines of tall walls or buildings receding behind them.

At the **center front** stands **Neo (Keanu Reeves)**. He is wearing a long black trench coat made of leather-like material, reaching almost to his ankles, and black pants. His shirt underneath is dark, and he has a gun holster visible at his sides. He is wearing **small, oval-shaped black sunglasses** and has a neutral, slightly tense expression. His arms hang by his sides.

To **Neo’s right** (the viewer’s left) stands **Trinity (Carrie-Anne Moss)**. She is also wearing an entirely black outfit, tight-fitting and shiny, like latex or vinyl. She has short-cropped hair, also wears dark oval sunglasses, and her stance is confident and slightly angled forward. Her right hand is partially visible near her waist, and she appears ready for action.

To **Neo’s left** (the viewer’s right) stands **Morpheus (Laurence Fishburne)**. He is bald and wears **small, round sunglasses** with dark lenses. His clothing consists of a long, textured purple coat with a high collar, and a dark tie or shirt underneath. His posture is upright, hands down by his sides, looking directly forward.

Further to **Morpheus’s left**, slightly behind the others, is **Cypher (Joe Pantoliano)**. He is also dressed in dark clothing with a coat, wearing sunglasses, and standing slightly in the background, with his body turned slightly to the side.

Above them, at the top of the poster, appears the **movie title**:

> **THE MATRIX**

The title is written in large, capitalized, light blue letters, with a slightly segmented, digital-looking typeface.

Below the title, along the **bottom edge of the poster**, is the standard **credit block** with actor names, director, producers, and other production information, all in small white or light-gray font. The **Warner Bros. logo** and the **Village Roadshow Pictures logo** are also located near the bottom.

No weapons are directly visible being held in their hands in this poster version. The overall composition is symmetrical, with Neo at the center, Morpheus and Trinity flanking him, and Cypher slightly behind. The entire group faces the viewer directly. The lighting highlights the characters sharply against the desaturated background.

7.text_description:
The poster shows several people standing in the middle of a city street that is partially submerged in water. The street is wide and straight, stretching back toward the horizon. The tall buildings on both sides are modern, made of glass and concrete, and they appear to rise vertically but also curve slightly inward at the top, suggesting that the city itself is folding or bending. The pavement in the foreground is dark, reflecting light as if wet.

There are six figures visible, standing in a loose formation, facing slightly different directions. The central figure, Leonardo DiCaprio’s character, is positioned in front, slightly separated from the others. He is wearing a dark suit and holding a pistol in his right hand, which hangs by his side. The other figures — also in formal or casual business attire — are scattered behind and beside him. One of them, to the left, also appears to be holding a weapon.

In the background, cars are partly submerged in the flooded street, and the water level reaches about halfway up their doors. The light in the scene is dim, bluish-gray, resembling early morning or dusk. The title **“INCEPTION”** is written in bold red capital letters near the bottom center of the poster. Above it, in smaller white text, is the tagline: **“YOUR MIND IS THE SCENE OF THE CRIME.”** Near the top, the actor names appear in white, spread out across the width of the poster: *Leonardo DiCaprio, Ken Watanabe, Joseph Gordon-Levitt, Marion Cotillard, Ellen Page, Tom Hardy, Cillian Murphy, Tom Berenger, and Michael Caine.*

At the very bottom, there is standard billing text with credits and the release information, also in white. The overall composition centers on the figures and the warped city background, giving equal prominence to both the people and the surreal cityscape.

9.poster_name: Star Wars: Episode IV - A New Hope (1977)
9.text_description:
The poster shows a large, dramatic composition centered around Luke Skywalker standing in the foreground. He is positioned slightly to the left of center, wearing a white tunic and tan pants, holding a raised lightsaber above his head with both hands. The lightsaber emits a bright, vertical blue beam of light extending upward toward the top of the image.

Beside Luke, slightly lower and to his left, stands Princess Leia. She is wearing a flowing white dress with a high slit, one leg visible, and she is holding a small blaster in her right hand, pointed outward. Her stance is dynamic, leaning slightly forward.

Behind and above them looms a large, semi-transparent depiction of Darth Vader’s helmeted face, dominating the upper part of the image. The blue lightsaber beam extends upward into the center of Vader’s mask, aligning roughly between his eyes.

In the background to the right and left, there are several spacecraft engaged in combat — X-wings and TIE fighters — with bright laser streaks crossing the space. Near the lower portion of the poster, slightly to the right, the two droids C-3PO and R2-D2 stand together, both facing outward.

At the bottom, the title **“STAR WARS”** appears in large, block capital letters, spanning the width of the image. Above or around it, smaller credit text lists names of cast and crew. The background shows outer space — dark, dotted with stars — and parts of a planet’s surface visible toward the lower edge, giving the impression of looking up from a planetary horizon.

10.poster_name: Star Wars: Episode V - The Empire Strikes Back (1980)
10.text_description:
The original theatrical release poster of **Star Wars: Episode V – The Empire Strikes Back (1980)** designed by **Roger Kastel** depicts a dramatic composition centered around the main characters and key visual motifs from the film.

At the center of the poster, **Han Solo and Princess Leia** dominate the image. Han is shown holding Leia in his arms in a romantic, almost embrace-like pose — her head tilted back, their faces close together, evoking the sense of intimacy between them. Han is dressed in his familiar outfit with dark jacket and trousers, while Leia wears her white outfit with cape-like folds.

Behind and above them, **Darth Vader’s helmeted face** looms large, occupying a significant portion of the upper poster. His mask and triangular mouthpiece are rendered prominently, and his presence forms a dark backdrop for the rest of the composition.

Below the central figures, **Luke Skywalker** appears on the left side, mounted on a **tauntaun**, wearing his snow gear with goggles and helmet — a reference to the icy planet Hoth. Near him, there are additional smaller figures of **Rebel soldiers** and **Imperial walkers (AT-ATs)** advancing in the snowy battlefield, with laser fire visible in the scene.

In the lower region of the poster, the **Millennium Falcon** is depicted in flight, pursued by **TIE fighters**, with streaks suggesting motion and combat.

At the top, the title text reads **“The Empire Strikes Back”** in large bold letters, contained within a rectangular frame that angles upward on the right side, matching the film’s logo design. The words **“Star Wars”** appear smaller above it.

At the bottom, the **credit block** lists production information — including **Lucasfilm**, **20th Century Fox**, and the main cast names such as **Mark Hamill, Harrison Ford, Carrie Fisher, Billy Dee Williams, Anthony Daniels, David Prowse, Kenny Baker, Peter Mayhew, and Frank Oz** — followed by the **director and producer credits**.

The overall layout balances the towering image of Vader above, the romantic pair at center, and the action scenes and spacecraft below, integrating character portraits and action vignettes into a single layered composition.

14.poster_name: Harry Potter and the Chamber of Secrets (2002)
14.text_description:
The poster shows several main characters arranged in a dense, fantasy-like composition centered around Harry Potter.

Harry is in the foreground, facing forward, slightly turned to his left. He is wearing round glasses and his Hogwarts school robe, which has a Gryffindor crest on the chest. He is holding a silver sword — the Sword of Godric Gryffindor — angled diagonally across his body, with its blade reflecting light. His expression is serious and focused.

To Harry’s left (on the viewer’s right side of the poster) is Hermione Granger, slightly behind him, visible from the waist up. She is also wearing Hogwarts robes and looking alert, with her hair loose and wavy. To Harry’s right (on the viewer’s left) stands Ron Weasley, also in school robes, his mouth slightly open and eyes wide as if startled.

Behind the three, farther back and above them, is Dobby the house-elf, looking downward with large eyes and long ears. In the background, there is also a faint figure of Professor Dumbledore, his long silver hair and beard visible, watching over them. The environment around the characters is a dimly lit stone chamber with greenish tones, suggestive of the Chamber of Secrets itself. On one side of the background there is the carved serpent head associated with Salazar Slytherin, its mouth open, and parts of snake-like patterns or stone columns visible.

At the bottom portion of the poster, the movie’s title *Harry Potter and the Chamber of Secrets* is written in its distinctive stylized golden font. Beneath it are the credits, including cast and production details, in small text. The release information (“November 15”) appears toward the bottom. The overall layout places Harry and the sword as the visual center, surrounded by his friends, mystical figures, and the chamber’s dark architecture.

15.text_description:
The poster shows Marty McFly standing in front of the DeLorean time machine. He is positioned slightly to the left of center, facing toward the viewer but glancing over his right shoulder toward the car. Marty is lifting up the front edge of his sunglasses with his left hand and looking at his wristwatch on his right arm. He is wearing a red puffer vest over a blue denim jacket, a checkered shirt underneath, jeans, and white sneakers. His right foot is partly inside the open gull-wing door of the DeLorean, which is raised above him.

The DeLorean’s headlights are on, casting a bright beam of light that illuminates the lower area of the poster. The car’s rear tires appear to be just off the ground, leaving behind two parallel trails of fire that extend diagonally toward the bottom edge of the image, suggesting motion or recent acceleration. The background transitions from dark blue at the top to a bright orange-yellow glow around the car.

Above Marty and the car, the movie title “BACK TO THE FUTURE” appears in large, slanted lettering, with the words arranged in two lines — “BACK TO THE” above “FUTURE.” The letters are colored in a gradient of yellow to red, with a stylized arrow extending backward from the word “BACK.” Near the top, above the title, the text reads “STEVEN SPIELBERG presents” and “A ROBERT ZEMECKIS Film.” At the bottom, there is standard billing information in small white text, including the names of the main cast, director, producers, and technical credits, along with the Universal Pictures logo.

17.poster_name: The Lord of the Rings: The Return of the King (2003)
17.text_description:
The poster shows several of the main characters arranged in a layered composition suggesting an impending battle or climax.

In the foreground, **Aragorn** is dominant at the center. He is seen from the waist up, slightly turned to his right but looking forward with an intense expression. He holds a **sword (Andúril)** in front of him, its blade vertical and gleaming, gripped in both hands. His hair is long and loose, and he wears dark battle armor with a silver emblem visible on his chest.

Behind and slightly above him to the left stands **Frodo Baggins**, his face lit, eyes wide and anxious. He is holding the **One Ring** dangling from a chain in his right hand, lifted slightly as if contemplating it. Just below and behind him on the left is **Samwise Gamgee**, looking determined, gazing toward the same direction as Frodo, his face dirty and tense.

To Aragorn’s right side, slightly higher, is **Gandalf the White**, shown from the waist up, holding his **staff** in his right hand and his **sword Glamdring** in the left. His long white hair and beard are distinct, and his expression is serious, directed forward.

In the upper right area of the poster, there is a large image of **Gollum**, his head and shoulders visible, looking downward with a sly, tense expression. Below him, to the lower right, **Éowyn** appears in armor with her long blond hair visible, holding a **sword** in a defensive or ready posture. Next to her, slightly behind, **Legolas** and **Gimli** appear—Legolas aiming his **bow** and Gimli holding his **axe**.

In the background, at the lower part of the image, there is a **battle scene** with many mounted soldiers, flags, and spears—suggesting the **armies of men** preparing for war. In the far distance, faintly visible, is a dark **fortress-like structure**, possibly **Mordor or Minas Tirith**, with fiery or smoke-like light behind it.

At the bottom center of the poster, the title **“The Lord of the Rings: The Return of the King”** is printed in large gold letters. Beneath it, in smaller type, are the release details and production credits.

19.poster_name: The Lord of the Rings: The Fellowship of the Ring (2001)
19.text_description:
The original theatrical release poster of *The Lord of the Rings: The Fellowship of the Ring* (2001) prominently features a large, close-up image of Frodo Baggins in the upper center area. He is holding the One Ring in his open palm, which glows faintly with warm light. His face is serious, and his curly hair frames it. He wears the Elven cloak, and the light emphasizes his eyes and hand.

Behind and around Frodo, several other main characters appear in a layered composition. Directly behind him on the left side is Gandalf the Grey, shown from the waist up, holding his staff in one hand and looking slightly downward or to the side. To Frodo’s right stands Aragorn, shown with his sword drawn and his face partially shadowed. Further behind, slightly smaller, are Legolas with his bow raised, Gimli holding his axe, and Arwen, whose long dark hair and Elven features are visible.

At the lower part of the poster, the rest of the Fellowship is shown on horseback or walking in a horizontal line, appearing small in scale against a misty, mountainous landscape. The background fades into a warm golden tone, suggesting dawn or a glowing light over the land.

The film’s title, *The Lord of the Rings: The Fellowship of the Ring*, is written in the lower portion of the poster in gold serif lettering, with “The Lord of the Rings” much larger than the subtitle beneath it. Above the title is the tagline: “Power can be held in the smallest of things.”

Below the title, in smaller text, are the production credits, studio logos, and the release year “2001” written at the bottom. The entire composition is vertically oriented, centered on Frodo’s hand and the Ring.
